title: Reserve Force Management
summary: Maintaining uncommitted forces to exploit opportunities or counter threats.
details: Never commit all forces simultaneously. Maintain a reserve to: exploit enemy weakness, counter unexpected threats, reinforce success, or cover retreat. Reserve size depends on situation: larger against uncertain enemy, smaller when objectives require maximum force. Commit reserve decisively when the moment is right - hesitation wastes it. Once committed, reconstitute reserve from units completing objectives.
annotations: The commander without reserve has lost control of the battle.
Reserve is the commander's personal weapon.
